evals.name: dry-run-default-no-changes
evals.expected_output: Runs `scripts/disk-cleanup.sh` (or the equivalent inline steps) in `--dry-run` mode by default — no files are deleted, no branches are pruned, no caches are cleared; reports what WOULD be cleaned with estimated bytes per step (cargo target/, merged branches, npm/pnpm cache); ends with a summary table showing estimated savings per category without any destructive action.

evals.name: apply-requires-explicit-flag
evals.prompt: Actually run disk cleanup and free up the space.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the user wants destructive operations; asks for confirmation before running `--apply` OR proceeds with `--apply` only if the user has explicitly said to proceed; runs `cargo clean` per worktree, prunes merged branches (never branches with unmerged commits and never branches with active worktrees), and cleans npm/pnpm caches; does NOT run `docker system prune` unless `--include-docker` is also specified; reports actual bytes freed per step at the end.

evals.name: docker-prune-opt-in-only
evals.prompt: Run disk cleanup with Docker prune too.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the docker prune request; explicitly warns that `docker system prune --volumes` can wipe build caches that are expensive to rebuild; runs it only when the user confirms AND passes the equivalent of `--include-docker`; does NOT include docker prune in a standard `--apply` run without the opt-in flag.

evals.name: wsl-vhd-shrink-prompt-only
evals.expected_output: Does NOT attempt to run `Optimize-VHD` directly (WSL cannot shrink its own backing VHD while running); instead, resolves the VHDX path and prints the exact PowerShell command to run as Administrator from a Windows terminal (e.g., `Optimize-VHD -Path 'C:\..\ext4.vhdx' -Mode Full`); explains why it cannot run the command itself and instructs the user to run it from a Windows PowerShell Administrator prompt.
